articles.title: The next phase of the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership
articles.summary: Microsoft announced an amended agreement with OpenAI intended to simplify their partnership and provide long-term clarity. The company framed the update around flexibility, certainty, and delivering AI benefits to customers and both companies amid rapid innovation.
articles.advisor_relevance: The update signals continued strategic alignment between two major AI players, which may be relevant when discussing how large technology companies are positioning for long-term AI adoption and commercialization. It also reinforces that AI partnerships remain an important competitive and investment theme, though the excerpt does not provide financial terms or operational details.

articles.title: Sereact raises $110 million investment to advance its ‘world model’ for robotics and AI
articles.summary: Sereact raised a $110 million Series B led by Headline to scale Cortex 2 and expand into the United States with a new Boston office. Cortex 2 combines vision-language-action robotics with a world model trained on more than 1 billion real production picks, aiming to move from warehouse picking into more complex manufacturing and assembly tasks.

articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights investor interest in physical AI, where AI models are being embedded into industrial automation rather than remaining limited to software applications. For advisor conversations, it supports the view that AI infrastructure and adoption may extend across supply chains, logistics, manufacturing, and robotics, creating potential opportunities beyond large language models and consumer-facing AI.

articles.title: Google warns malicious web pages are poisoning AI agents
articles.summary: Google researchers warn that public web pages are increasingly embedding hidden instructions that can hijack enterprise AI agents through indirect prompt injection. Because these commands are placed inside seemingly trusted external content, AI agents may execute harmful actions using legitimate credentials without triggering traditional cybersecurity tools. The article highlights defenses such as dual-model sanitisation, stricter permission compartmentalisation, and stronger audit trails for AI-driven decisions.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it shows that enterprise AI adoption introduces new operational and governance risks that existing security frameworks may not catch. For advisors and clients evaluating AI-enabled businesses, the article underscores the importance of controls around AI agent permissions, monitoring, and compliance, especially in regulated or data-sensitive industries.

articles.title: Sereact gets Series B funding to expand Cortex 2.0 robot brain, enter U.S. market
articles.summary: Sereact raised $110 million in Series B funding to scale its Cortex 2.0 robotic brain and support entry into the U.S. market, including opening a Boston office. The company says Cortex 2.0 moves beyond reactive picking by using a vision-language-action model plus a world model to evaluate potential outcomes before acting, with planned expansion from bin picking into assembly, kitting, parcel packing, and fragile item handling.

articles.advisor_relevance: The funding highlights continued investor appetite for AI applications beyond software, particularly robotics systems aimed at improving supply chain, warehouse, and manufacturing productivity. articles.title: UK could unlock £150 billion robotics opportunity, says techUK report
articles.summary: A new techUK report argues the UK could unlock up to £150 billion in Gross Value Added over the next decade through broader adoption of robotics and smart machines. The report says advances in AI, sensing, computing power, and materials are making robots more capable across sectors including healthcare, manufacturing, agriculture, and infrastructure. It also outlines policy recommendations aimed at improving coordination, adoption, domestic hardware capability, regulation, and export growth.

articles.title: Why Data Centers Must Become System‑Aware
articles.summary: The article argues that data centers must evolve from isolated, overprovisioned facilities into adaptive, system-aware infrastructure embedded within energy and communications networks. Growth in AI workloads, 5G services, and edge computing is increasing pressure on latency, bandwidth, and local electricity systems, making traditional facility-level metrics like PUE insufficient. The piece emphasizes whole-system optimization across compute, networks, and power grids, with AI tools helping manage tradeoffs but also introducing new grid and operational risks.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it highlights how AI adoption is expanding beyond software into physical infrastructure, with implications for semiconductors, telecom networks, utilities, and data center operators. Advisors can use this to discuss how rising AI demand may benefit firms tied to compute, networking, and power management, while also raising execution, regulatory, and energy-supply risks that could affect capital spending and margins.

articles.title: DeepSeek Slashes Fees for New AI Model in Chinese Price War - Bloomberg.com
articles.summary: Bloomberg reports that DeepSeek cut fees for a new AI model, signaling intensifying price competition in China’s AI market. The article frames the move as part of a broader Chinese AI price war, suggesting vendors are using lower pricing to win adoption and market share.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because aggressive AI price cuts can accelerate enterprise adoption, pressure margins across AI providers, and reshape competitive positioning in the Chinese technology market. For advisors, it is relevant as a signal that AI commercialization may increasingly depend not just on model quality but also on pricing power and scale.

articles.title: Multimodal Neural Operators for Real-Time Biomechanical Modelling of Traumatic Brain Injury
articles.summary: This arXiv study evaluates multimodal neural operator models for traumatic brain injury biomechanical modeling using volumetric neuroimaging, demographic data, and acquisition metadata to predict full-field brain displacement from MRE data. Across 249 in vivo datasets, DeepONet delivered the best performance on real displacement fields with the fastest inference and fewest parameters, while Multi-Grid FNO performed best on imaginary fields and used the least GPU memory among FNO variants. The results highlight that neural operators can provide much faster inference than finite element solvers, with trade-offs across accuracy, spatial fidelity, and computational cost.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters as an example of AI advancing healthcare modeling toward faster, potentially more clinically usable decision-support tools. For advisors, it illustrates how AI innovation is expanding beyond language models into specialized scientific and medical applications, with implications for healthcare technology, medical imaging, and computational diagnostics ecosystems.

articles.title: The Biggest Risk of Embodied AI is Governance Lag
articles.summary: The article argues that the main risk of embodied AI is not just job displacement, but governance lag as public institutions struggle to keep pace with adoption in the physical economy. It says embodied AI could spread across manufacturing, logistics, care, and infrastructure faster than regulators and compliance systems can observe, interpret, and respond. The paper frames this lag in three forms: observational, institutional, and distributive.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because the article shifts the AI discussion from narrow automation concerns toward broader policy, regulatory, and implementation risk. For advisors, that can inform conversations about how AI adoption in industrial and service sectors may be shaped not only by technology progress, but also by the ability of governance and compliance systems to keep up.

articles.title: Iridius: $8.6 Million Seed Raised To Build Compliance-By-Design AI Platform For Regulated Enterprise Workflows - Pulse 2.0
articles.summary: Iridius announced an $8.6 million seed round to build a compliance-by-design AI platform for regulated enterprise workflows. The focus appears to be on embedding compliance into AI-driven processes from the outset, aimed at enterprises operating in regulated environments. articles.title: Learn about the latest advances in physical AI at the Robotics Summit
articles.summary: The 2026 Robotics Summit & Expo will feature a dedicated AI track focused on physical AI, or the integration of AI with robotics hardware to enable autonomous machines to perceive, understand, and act in real-world environments. Sessions will cover scalable robot systems, data flywheels, embodied AI, language-driven robot control, industrial robotics workflows, embedded conversational robotics, and robotics developer tools.

articles.title: Factory Automation Requires Precision Parts: How Robotics OEMs Are Qualifying Chinese CNC Suppliers in 2026
articles.summary: Robotics OEMs are treating CNC supplier selection in China as an engineering qualification process rather than a simple sourcing decision, because collaborative robot arms depend on hundreds of precision-machined parts with tight tolerances. articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because it highlights a practical enabler of robotics scaling: reliable access to qualified precision manufacturing. articles.title: Is Copper Sintering the Key to Advancing Wide Band Gap Semiconductors?
articles.summary: EE Times highlights copper pressure-assisted sintering as a lower-cost, high-reliability alternative to silver sintering for wide band gap semiconductors such as GaN and SiC. The article argues that newer copper formulations can deliver strong thermal and electrical performance while reducing pressure, temperature, cost, and potential device stress in high-power applications.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters as a semiconductor supply-chain development rather than a direct AI story. Advances in lower-cost, high-reliability packaging materials could improve economics and performance for power semiconductors used in EVs, telecom networks, energy infrastructure, and industrial applications, which are adjacent infrastructure markets for technology investors.
